---
title: "Ramin Akbari"
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
Hey there! I am a Ph.D. student in Computer Science and Engineering at Michigan State University, advised by [Dr. Vishnu Boddeti](https://vishnu.boddeti.net/){.advisor-link}. I work on statistical machine learning with a primary focus on the mathematics of controlling representations. My research aims to develop algorithms that mitigate bias in AI models, improve fairness, protect data privacy, and advance safe and trustworthy AI systems.

I also have background in computational mechanics, specifically working with the eXtended Finite Element Method (XFEM), computational homogenization and the constitutive modeling of hyper-elastic material.

* **Ph.D. Student in Computer Science** | Michigan State University
* **M.Sc. in Structural Mechanics** | Sharif University of Technology
* **B.Sc. in Civil Engineering** | University of Tehran

<br>

<div class="button-container">
  <a href="https://github.com/ramin-akbari" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" ><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Q2kM_tcAAAAJ&hl=en" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i> Google Scholar</a>
  <a href="cv.pdf" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-file-earmark-person"></i> CV</a>
  <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/ramin-akbari-a7580025b/" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" ><i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i> LinkedIn</a>
  <a href="mailto:akbarigh@msu.edu" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" ><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i> Email</a>
</div>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](ramin2.jpg){width="85%" .rounded}
:::

:::
<br>

## 📢 News 

::: {.grid style="row-gap: 0.2rem;"}

::: {.g-col-2}
**Jan 2026**
:::
::: {.g-col-10}
Extended my internship at **Sartorius** for the spring semester.
:::
::: {.g-col-2}
**Sep 2025**
:::
::: {.g-col-10}
Our paper *Obliviator Reveals the Cost of Nonlinear Guardedness in Concept Erasure* was accepted to **NeurIPS 2025**!
:::
::: {.g-col-2}
**Aug 2025**
:::
::: {.g-col-10}
Started my internship at **Sartorius** in Ann Arbor.
:::
::: {.g-col-2}
**Jun 2025**
:::
::: {.g-col-10}
Our paper *Homomorphically Encrypted Biometric Template Fusion and Matching* was accepted to **TBIOM**!
:::

:::

## 📝 Selected Publications
::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](/posts/obliviator/teaser.png){width="100%" .rounded}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
<span style="font-size: 1.15em;">**Obliviator Reveals the Cost of Nonlinear Guardedness in Concept Erasure**</span><br>
<u>Ramin Akbari</u>\*, Milad Afshari\*, Vishnu Boddeti<br>
*Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), Dec 2025*

In this work, we present **Obliviator**, a method for performing concept erasure on pre-trained language models. Concept erasure poses two central challenges: first, capturing all types of dependencies between the concept and the learned representation; and second, keeping unrelated information intact. Obliviator addresses the shortcomings of current methods by guarding concepts against nonlinear adversaries while preserving more information for the same level of concept leakage compared to baselines, setting a formidable benchmark for the utility-erasure trade-off.


<div class="button-container">
  <a href="posts/obliviator/index.html" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" style=""><i class="bi bi-file-richtext"></i> Blog</a>
  <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.07529" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> arXiv</a>
  <a href="https://github.com/ramin-akbari/Obliviator" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> Code</a>
  <a href="https://openreview.net/forum?id=GcjpjIHDZn" class="btn btn-outline-success btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-journal-check"></i> OpenReview</a>
  <a href="/posts/obliviator/poster.png" class="btn btn-outline-success btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-easel"></i> Poster</a>
  <a href="posts/obliviator/nips-slides/index.html" class="btn btn-outline-success btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-file-slides"></i> Slides</a>
</div>
:::

:::
---
